---
title: ""
page-layout: full
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
toc-location: right
---

```{=html}
<div class="hero">
  <div class="hero-inner">
    <h1 class="hero-name">Curriculum Vitae</h1>
    <p class="hero-tagline">
      Dr. Cory William Dugan &nbsp;·&nbsp; PhD &nbsp;·&nbsp; AFHEA
    </p>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Cory Dugan, PhD

**Postdoctoral Research Fellow** | Pennington Biomedical Research Centre

Investigating iron regulation and physiological function through novel approaches in nutrition and metabolism.

[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/cory-dugan-phd/) | [Email](mailto:cory.dugan1@gmail.com)

---

## Education

### Doctor of Philosophy — Nutrition & Exercise Physiology
**The University of Western Australia** | 2020–2024

*PhD Title:* "Iron and women's health: A multidimensional exploration of iron deficiency, management, and treatment in active females"

**Skills:** Clinical Trial Management, Data Analysis, Meta-analysis, Project Management, R Programming

### Bachelor of Science (First Class Honours) — Bioenergetics
**The University of Western Australia** | 2018–2019

Thesis explored effects of simulated high altitude on blood glucose during exercise in type 1 diabetes. Altmetric score of 395 (top 5%).

### Bachelor of Science — Sport & Exercise Science
**The University of Western Australia** | 2018–2019

WAM: 88.25, GPA: 7.0

Highlight: Frank Pyke Memorial Scholarship. Key coursework included Advanced Exercise Physiology, Research Methods, and Data Analysis.

### Associate Fellowship (AFHEA) — Higher Education
**Advance HE** | 2022

Recognition for commitment to professional teaching and learning.

---

## Professional Experience

### Postdoctoral Research Fellow
**Pennington Biomedical Research Centre** | 2025–Present | Full-time

Focus on iron regulation and physiological function.

### Founder
**HolisticHer** | 2024–Present | Owner

Women's health coaching and wellness business.

### Director
**P∆R∆DOX Health & Wellbeing** | 2024–Present | Owner

### Adjunct Research Fellow
**Curtin University** | 2024–Present

Women's health research and grant writing.

### Research Fellow
**Monash University** | 2024 | Full-time

Clinical research in exercise physiology and micronutrient metabolism.

### Research Officer (Chief Investigator)
**University of Western Australia** | 2023–Present

Leading the DARP Trial with Prof. Paul Fournier.

### Associate Lecturer
**University of Western Australia** | 2022–2024

Lecturing across exercise physiology, bioenergetics, women's health, and professional business communication.

### Coordinating Physiologist & Lab Specialist
**UWA Exercise & Performance Centre** | 2022–2024 | Part-time

### Academic Tutor & Lab Demonstrator
**University of Western Australia** | 2021–2024 | Part-time

Across three UWA schools (Human Sciences, Medical, Business).

### Head Strength and Conditioning Coach
**DDFUA** | 2020–2023

### Umpire
**AFL** | 2017

AFLW games during inaugural year.

### Personal Trainer
**QV1** | 2017–2022

### Research Assistant
**University of Western Australia** | 2016–2017

Assisting Dr. Joanna Nicholas with body composition measurements.

### Tournament Director
**Australian Poker League** | 2015–2017

### Umpire
**WAFL** | 2015–2024

### Croupier
**Crown** | 2014–2015

---

## Teaching

| Unit | School | Role | Years |
|------|--------|------|-------|
| Applied Professional Business Communications (BUSN5100) | UWA Business School | Tutor & Lecturer | 2023–2025 |
| The Musculoskeletal System and Movement (SSEH1101) | UWA Human Sciences | Lab Demonstrator | 2021–2024 |
| Exercise Physiology (SSEH2260) | UWA Human Sciences | Tutor & Lecturer | 2021–2024 |
| Bioenergetics in Exercise, Nutrition and Energy Balance (SSEH3366) | UWA Human Sciences | Tutor & Lecturer | 2021–2024 |
| Issues in Women's Health Research (IMED3301) | UWA IMED | Tutor & Lecturer | 2021–2024 |
| Health and Fitness (SSEH1103) | UWA Human Sciences | Coordinator | 2021–2024 |
| Sport Physiology (SSEH3365) | UWA Human Sciences | Tutor & Lecturer | 2021–2024 |

---

## Supervision

| Student | Project Title | Level | Role | Years |
|---------|---------------|-------|------|-------|
| Ms. Kanon Uchiyama | Rest Break Frequency and Performance in Mining Simulations | Masters | Secondary Supervisor | 2022–Present |
| Mr. Samuel Kell | Exploring appetite suppression and oxidative stress after maximal sprint effort | Masters | Secondary Supervisor | 2023–Present |
| Ms. Caitlin Atwell | Dietary Iron and the Elite Dancer | Honours | Secondary Supervisor | 2023–Present |
| Mr. Arjan Ruba | Investigating hydration's impact on hypoglycaemia in type 1 diabetes during exercise | Masters | Secondary Supervisor | 2024–Present |

---

## Honours & Awards

| Award | Year | Type | Organisation | Amount |
|-------|------|------|-------------|--------|
| Wiley Top Downloaded Article (Transfusion) | 2025 | Prize | Wiley | |
| BJSM PhD Academy Award | 2024 | Award | BJSM | |
| NATA Travel Grant | 2023 | Grant | NATA | $5,000 |
| NHMRC Investigator Grant: DARP | 2023 | Grant | | $60,000 |
| Editors Choice: Transfusion Evidence Library | 2023 | Prize | Wiley | |
| UWA Teaching Excellence (Early Career) | 2023 | Award | UWA | |
| Aus.Iron Travel Grant | 2022 | Grant | | $2,000 |
| Associate Fellowship AFHEA | 2022 | Award | Advance HE | |
| NATA Best Poster - Oral Communications | 2021 | Prize | NATA | |
| NATA Presentation Prize | 2021 | Prize | NATA | |
| Wiley Top Downloaded Article (Anaesthesia) | 2021 | Prize | Wiley | |
| Australian Government RTP Scholarship + Stipend | 2020 | Scholarship | | $190,500 |
| Telethon Kids Institute Travel Grant | 2020 | Grant | | $3,000 |
| Pharmacosmos PhD Top Up Scholarship | 2019 | Scholarship | | $30,000 |
| Pharmacosmos RCT Grant | 2019 | Grant | | $555,460 |
| Life Membership for Outstanding Service | 2019 | Prize | DDFUA | |
| Telethon Kids Institute Seeding Grant | 2019 | Grant | | $17,000 |
| Frank Pyke Memorial Scholarship | 2018 | Scholarship | UWA | $5,000 |
| NHMRC CRE T1D Honours Scholarship | 2018 | Scholarship | | $5,000 |
| UWA Ethics Committee Application of the Year | 2018 | Honour | UWA | $5,000 |

---

## Certifications

| Certification | Organisation | Year |
|---------------|-------------|------|
| Standard Mental Health First Aider | Mental Health First Aid International | 2024 |
| R—Data Use in Science (High Distinction, SCIE4401) | UWA | 2022 |
| Statistics in R | Australian and NZ Society for Immunology | 2021 |
| Introduction to R & Statistics in R | UWA | 2021 |
| Meta-analysis Statistics | Salis Institute | 2020 |
| Systematic review and meta-analysis | Cochrane | 2020 |
| Systematically Review the Literature | Salis Institute | 2020 |
| Good Clinical Practice (GCP) | WAHTN | 2019 |
| Clinical Research- Conducting Pragmatic RCTs | Western University | 2019 |
| Standard First Aid CPR and AED | Sports Medicine Australia | 2017 |
| Certificate IV Fitness | North Metropolitan TAFE | 2017 |
| Certificate III Fitness | North Metropolitan TAFE | 2017 |
| Casino Gaming Workers/Card Dealer License | Australian Poker League | 2015 |
| Responsible service of alcohol | Crown Resorts | 2014 |
| Responsible service of gambling | Crown Resorts | 2014 |

---

## Publications

1. MacLean, B., Lim, J., Fuller, J., et al. & **Dugan, C.** (2025). Community screening for iron deficiency in reproductive aged women. *Vox Sanguinis, 120*(1), 22-31.

2. **Dugan, C.**, Scott, C., Abeysiri, S., et al. (2021). The need to screen for anemia in exercising women. *Medicine, 100*(39), e27271.

3. **Dugan, C.**, Cabolis, K., Miles, L. F., & Richards, T. (2022). Systematic review and meta-analysis of IV iron therapy for adults with non-anaemic iron deficiency. *J Cachexia Sarcopenia Muscle, 13*(6), 2637-2649.

4. MacLean, B., Fuller, J., Lim, J., **Dugan, C.**, & Richards, T. (2025). Greater prevalence of anaemia and heavy menstrual bleeding... *British J Haematology, 206*(5), 1479-84. (Pre-Print)

5. **Dugan, C.** (2024). Iron and women's health [Doctoral Dissertation]. UWA Research Repository.

6. Attwell, C., **Dugan, C.**, McKay, A. K., et al. (2022). Dietary iron and the elite dancer. *Nutrients, 14*(9), 1936.

7. Attwell, C., McKay, A., Sim, M., **Dugan, C.**, et al. (2023). Timing is everything... iron supplementation in ballet dancers. *European J Sport Science, 23*(12), 2275-2282.

8. Lim, J., MacLean, B., **Dugan, C.**, et al. (2024). TikTok videos on iron deficiency. *Healthcare*. (Under Review)

9. **Dugan, C.**, Peeling, P., Burden, R., & Richards, T. (2024). Efficacy of iron supplementation on physical capacity: Protocol for an IPD meta-analysis. *Systematic Reviews, 13*(1), 182.

10. **Dugan, C.**, & Hensen, W. (2025). Ironing out iron deficiency in female athletes. *Sports Injury Bulletin, 22*(1).

11. **Dugan, C.**, MacLean, B., Cabolis, K., et al. (2021). The misogyny of iron deficiency. *Anaesthesia, 76*, 56-62.

12. **Dugan, C.**, & Hennigar, S. (2025). Carbon monoxide inhalation to increase haemoglobin mass. *BJSM, 59*(11), 749-750.

13. Clarke, L., Froessler, B., ..., **Dugan, C.**, et al. (2025). Iron optimisation in pregnancy. *Internal Medicine Journal, 55*(2), 300-307.

14. Uchiyama, K., King, J., Wallman, K., Taggart, S., **Dugan, C.**, & Girard, O. (2022). Rest break frequency and duration: a mining simulation study. *European J Applied Physiology, 122*(9), 2087-2097.

15. Peeling, P., McKay, A., McCormick, R., **Dugan, C.**, & Sim, M. (2022). Iron and the Athlete. *The Nutrition-Change*.

16. Abeysiri, S., **Dugan, C.**, Raobaikady, R., Richards, T., & Scott, C. (2021). The need to screen for anaemia in female populations. *J Science and Medicine in Sport*.

17. **Dugan, C.** (2024). Iron and women's health [PhD Academy Award]. *BJSM, 58*(23), 1466-1468.

18. Davies, A. T., Devlin, P. M., **Dugan, C.**, et al. (2023). Non-ESA non-iron therapies for anaemia: A scoping review. *Transfusion, 63*(4), 849-860.

19. **Dugan, C. W.**, et al. (2022). Effects of simulated high altitude on blood glucose during exercise in T1D. *JCEM, 107*(5), 1375-1382.

20. **Dugan, C.**, Peeling, P., et al. (2025). IRONWOMAN trial. *BJSM, 59*(13), 921-930.

21. **Dugan, C.**, Simpson, A., Peeling, P., et al. (2023). Perceived impact of iron deficiency in exercising females. *Patient Preference and Adherence, 17*, 2097-2108.

22. Richards, T., Wijaya, L. K., Lim, J., **Dugan, C.**, & Dahly, D. (2025). IV Iron-induced hypophosphatemia in surgical patients. *JAMA Network Open, 8*(4), e253093.

23. James, C., **Dugan, C. W.**, Boyd, C., et al. (2024). Temporal tracking of cysteine 34 oxidation. *European J Applied Physiology, 124*(9), 2639-2650.

24. **Dugan, C.**, Peeling, P., Davies, A., et al. (2024). Menorrhagia, iron deficiency, and anaemia in active females. *JSAMS, 27*(7), 437-450.

25. Devlin, P., Davies, A., **Dugan, C.**, et al. (2022). Non-ESA non-iron therapies protocol. *BMJ Open, 12*(4), e059059.

---

## Conference Proceedings

Presentations across conferences including NATA, Aus.Iron, ACSM, ANZSBT, Sports Dietitians Australia, and Pennington Biomedical Research Center Symposium (2018–2025).

---

## Research Funding

| Grant | Year | Funder | Amount (AUD) |
|-------|------|--------|-------------|
| Pharmacosmos RCT Grant | 2019 | Pharmacosmos | $550,000 |
| RTP PhD Studentship | 2019 | UWA | $180,000 |
| NHMRC Investigator Grant DARP | 2023 | NHMRC | $60,000 |
| Pharmacosmos PhD Top Up | 2019 | Pharmacosmos | $30,000 |
| Telethon Kids Seeding Grant | 2019 | Telethon Kids | $17,000 |
| NATA Travel Grant | 2023 | NATA | $5,000 |
| Frank Pyke Scholarship | 2018 | UWA | $5,000 |
| NHMRC CRE T1D Honours Scholarship | 2018 | NHMRC | $5,000 |
| UWA Ethics Application of Year | 2018 | UWA | $5,000 |
| Telethon Kids Travel Grant | 2019 | Telethon Kids | $3,000 |
| Aus.Iron Travel Grant | 2022 | Aus.Iron | $2,000 |

---

## Invited Presentations

1. Iron and women's health, 2024, Multiple venues
2. Women's health student research symposium, 2024, UWA
3. Issues in women's health guest lecture, 2024, UWA
4. Free iron deficiency screening, 2023, 10,000+ females
5. Novel research in type 1 diabetes, 2021, Type 1 Diabetes Family Centre
6. Guest lectures across exercise physiology and bioenergetics, 2021–2024, UWA

---

## Peer Review

**Editorial Roles:** Academic Editor (PLOS One), Guest Editor (Women's Health)

**Paid Reviewer:** Journal of Medical Economics (2025), BMC Public Health (2025)

**Journals Reviewed For:** PLOS One, Women's Health, Journal of Medical Economics, BMC Public Health, and numerous other peer-reviewed journals.

---

## Media Engagement

50+ media appearances across outlets including: ABC Radio, Sydney Morning Herald, Brisbane Times, Medical News Today, MSN, Healio, EurekAlert, US Pharmacist, Medpage Today, Sports Injury Bulletin, UWA News, Medical Life Sciences News, and Times of India.

---

## Policy Citations

1. **WHO (2025):** Guidance on implementing patient blood management. Cited the JCSM 2022 meta-analysis.

2. **OSHA (2024):** Heat Injury and Illness Prevention. Cited the EJAP 2022 mining study.

3. **Public Health Ontario (2023):** Interventions to Mitigate Heat-related Harms. Cited the EJAP 2022 study.

---

## Service & Engagement

### Industry / Government Collaborations
- Iron optimisation in pregnancy HOW Collaborative (2025)
- PowerfulHer Podcast (2025)

### Community Engagement
- Issues in women's health guest lecture (2024)
- Free iron deficiency screening (2023) — 10,000+ females
- Free anaemia screening (2021)
- Every Woman Triathlon screening (2021)

### Professional Networks
- WANFLUA member (2015–Present)
- DDFUA member (2009–Present, Life Member 2019)

### Online Communities
- Facebook group admin: Iron Deficiency Anaemia Women's Group (34,000+ members)
- Facebook group admin: Anaemia Education (5,000+ members)

### Mentoring & Leadership
- STRIVE mentor (2021)

### Consultation
- The misogyny of iron deficiency consultancy (2020)

---

## References

Available upon request
